65.reason: [The 'push' function receives 'top' by value, so modifying 'top' inside 'push' doesn't affect the caller. However, here 'top' is incremented regardless of whether the push succeeded or failed (e.g., when stack is full). This leads to incorrect top tracking and potential undefined behavior.]

72.reason: [The 'pop' function only prints "error " on underflow but still returns 0. The caller decrements 'top' even when the pop fails (i.e., when stack is empty), causing 'top' to go below -1 and leading to further errors. Also, successful pop should decrement 'top', but this happens even on error.]
